summary: RC-06 BATCH 2 — E2E GUI validation for operational roles: Técnico Mecânico, Técnico Elétrico, Lubrificador, Operador, Visualizador. All 5 profiles logged in successfully via GUI (empresa → org → email/pwd), all sidebar RBAC rules matched code, all role labels correct in the sidebar bottom (uses ROLE_LABELS map). Two complete 2-step Solicitar Serviço submissions succeeded end-to-end (Tec Mec generated OS #2026-00062, Operador generated OS #2026-00063). RBAC on /admin/* and /master/* correctly renders 'access-restricted'. Two real bugs found on Visualizador: (1) '+ NOVA OS' and '+ NOVA INSPEÇÃO' buttons are visible/clickable although Visualizador should be read-only; (2) route /solicitar is not blocked for Visualizador (no 'allow' prop on ProtectedRoute).

ui_bugs.issue: Button '+ Nova OS' at line 3705 (data-testid='add-os-btn') is unconditionally rendered. Visualizador role sees and can click it. Spec: Visualizador must have NO create actions.
Button '+ Nova Inspeção' at line 4879 is unconditionally rendered. Visualizador role sees and can click it. Spec: Visualizador must have NO create actions.

ui_bugs.component: Route /solicitar — ProtectedRoute missing role allow list
ui_bugs.issue: Line 9976: <ProtectedRoute><SolicitacaoServicoPage/> has no 'allow' prop, so Visualizador can navigate to /solicitar and open the wizard. Should restrict to operacional roles + operador.

ui_bugs.component: Sidebar — Estoque visible to operational roles
ui_bugs.issue: 'Estoque' menu item appears in sidebar for tec_mecanico, tec_eletrico, lubrificador, operador (line 1632). The RC-06 spec for BATCH 2 lists only Minha Jornada, Ativos, Inspeções, Solicitar Serviço, Scanner, Ronda as the expected sidebar for these roles. If Estoque is intended for operational roles, spec should be updated; otherwise Estoque should be gated by !isOperacional.

issues: After logout, the empresa autocomplete input is cleared visually but calling page.type() with new text APPENDS to a lingering internal 'empresaBusca' state on some sessions, producing e.g. 'ASTEC CedroSTEC'. Reproduced when clicking Sair from operational profile — non-blocking (a fill('') fixes it) but users might see stale text if they navigate back to /login without a full reload.

action_items: HIGH: Hide '+ Nova OS' (data-testid='add-os-btn') for Visualizador role (and any other read-only role). Recommended gate: !isVisualizador && !isOperacional (operational roles already have their own Solicitar wizard).
HIGH: Hide '+ Nova Inspeção' button on /inspecoes for Visualizador role.

HIGH: Add allow prop to /solicitar route in App.js line 9976: <ProtectedRoute allow={['operador','tec_mecanico','tec_eletrico','instrumentista','lubrificador','tecnico','inspetor','supervisor','pcm','admin','master']}> so Visualizador and Gerente cannot access it.

MEDIUM: Decide whether 'Estoque' should be visible in the sidebar for operational profiles (Técnico/Lubrificador/Operador). Current code shows it (line 1632); RC-06 BATCH 2 spec does not list it. Either update spec or gate with !isOperacional.
LOW: On logout, clear the empresa autocomplete state in LoginPage to avoid stale 'ASTEC Cedro' remaining bound when the user re-enters /login.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js line 9976: /solicitar route missing 'allow' prop — any authenticated user (including Visualizador) can reach the SolicitacaoServicoPage. Contradicts the read-only intent of Visualizador.

App.js line 3705 (OS page 'Nova OS') and line 4879 (Inspeções 'Nova Inspeção'): create buttons are not role-gated. Even though Visualizador cannot see these pages in the sidebar for other read-only pages, /os and /inspecoes ARE in the Visualizador CONSULTA sidebar. So Visualizador sees create CTAs on both pages.

Sidebar Estoque visibility (App.js line 1632): unconditional inside MATERIAIS section. Compare with /sobressalentes and /paradas which are !isOperacional. If operational roles are meant to consult stock, current behavior is fine; otherwise fix.

LoginPage empresa autocomplete state persists across logout — resetting empresaBusca/orgId on unmount would prevent the visible 'ASTEC CedroSTEC' concatenation observed on re-navigating to /login without refresh.

success_rate.frontend: 100% login+navigation success. 5/5 profiles executed. 3 HIGH-priority Visualizador RBAC gaps found.

test_credentials: test.mec@maintrix.com/tec123 (Tec Mec), test.ele@maintrix.com/tec123 (Tec Ele), test.operador@maintrix.com/op123 (Operador) — from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Lubrificador and Visualizador test users were CREATED via POST /api/admin/users with master token, force_password_change flag flipped to false directly in MongoDB (no admin API to do this), TESTED via GUI, then DELETED via DELETE /api/admin/users/{id}. Cleanup complete: both users removed.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test users via API: test.lub@maintrix.com (role=lubrificador, id=f27fdf83-f1bf-4990-849b-72332d634ced) and test.viewer@maintrix.com (role=visualizador, id=f7d76bc3-cea2-404e-a24d-96a63b5a25f3). Both DELETED after testing. Two OS created by the Solicitar Serviço submissions (#2026-00062 for Tec Mec, #2026-00063 for Operador) remain in the database — expected behavior of the flow, not blocking; can be closed/cancelled via master if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: RC-06 BATCH 2 complete. All 5 operational-side profiles pass GUI login + sidebar RBAC + Acesso Restrito + role labels. Two 2-step Solicitar Serviço wizards submitted end-to-end (Tec Mec, Operador) → toast success + navigate('/'). Bugs to retest after fix: (a) Visualizador should NOT see '+ Nova OS' on /os nor '+ Nova Inspeção' on /inspecoes; (b) Visualizador should get access-restricted at /solicitar. Also verify Gerente (which is !isOperacional && !isAdmin && !isVisualizador) still sees Nova OS button — Gerente MUST retain create capability. Fix scope: only role-gate the two CTAs and add allow prop to /solicitar. To recreate test users: POST /api/admin/users with master token, then Mongo: db.users.updateMany({email:{$in:[...]}}, {$set:{force_password_change:false}}).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: sidebar-level RBAC hides menu items but individual pages retain their create CTAs (Nova OS, Nova Inspeção) without role checks. Because Visualizador's CONSULTA section EXPOSES /os and /inspecoes as read-only entry points, the user can reach them and see write-actions. Same pattern on /solicitar — the sidebar hides the item for Visualizador, but the route itself has no allow list, so direct URL entry works. Fix must be applied at BOTH layers (route + CTA visibility) for defense-in-depth.
